title: Palpitation Prevention: 11 Herbal Creams to Get You Started

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams are often used to help with palpitations, which are irregular heartbeats that can feel scary or uncomfortable. This is because the cream contains a plant extract called valerian root, which has been used for centuries to calm the body and mind. When applied to the skin, valerian root can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of palpitations. It does this by promoting relaxation and slowing down the heart rate, making it easier to breathe and feel calm. The cream can also help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitations. Additionally, valerian root has a natural sedative effect, which can help to regulate the heartbeat and prevent irregular rhythms. As a result, using Valeriana officinalis creams may help to reduce the frequency and severity of palpitations, allowing for a more peaceful and calm state of being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Centaurium erythraea herbal creams that help with palpitations, first, you'll need to dry the Centaurium erythraea plant. This can be done by tying the stems together and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the plant is completely dry, you'll need to make a herbal infusion by steeping 1 tablespoon of the dried plant in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes. After straining the mixture, combine the herbal infusion with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of 1:10. For example, mix 1 part of the herbal infusion with 10 parts of the carrier oil. Next, heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until the oil has cooled to a comfortable temperature. Finally, combine the infused oil with a cream base, such as shea butter or beeswax, in a ratio of 1:1, and mix until well combined. This Centaurium erythraea herbal cream can be applied topically to help alleviate palpitations and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata cream is a natural remedy that may help with palpitations. Palpitations are fast or irregular heartbeats that can be scary and uncomfortable. This cream is made from the herb Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower. For centuries, passionflower has been used to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to palpitations. The active compounds in passionflower, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, may help slow down the heart rate and reduce feelings of stress and anxiety. By applying the cream to the skin, the active compounds can be absorbed into the body and start working to calm the nervous system and reduce palpitations. Some people find that using Passiflora incarnata cream helps them feel more relaxed and in control, which can be especially helpful when they're experiencing palpitations.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Centaurium erythraea herbal creams for palpitations, first, you need to prepare the herb. Centaurium erythraea, also known as European centaury or centaury, can be dried and powdered. This can be done by spreading the leaves and flowers on a clean surface to dry completely in a warm and shaded area. Once dry, use a coffee grinder or mortar to finely powder the herb. Next, mix the powdered Centaurium erythraea with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:10 ratio, for example, 1 gram of the herb with 10 grams of oil. Add a small amount of beeswax to thicken the mixture and create a cream-like texture. Finally, heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled and thickened. The herbal cream is now ready to use for its potential benefits in managing palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a type of plant extract that is often used to help with palpitations. This is because it has a calming effect on the body and mind. When you have a palpitation, it means that your heart beats too fast or irregularly. This can be caused by stress, anxiety, or other emotional factors. Melissa officinalis cream can help to calm your nervous system and slow down your heart rate. Applying the cream to your skin can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which in turn can help to regulate your heart rate. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Centaurium erythraea herbal cream for palpitation, start by collecting the dried flowers of the plant, also known as the European gentian. You can buy dried Centaurium erythraea flowers from a health food store or online. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in a heat-proof bowl with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil. Place the bowl in a saucepan of simmering water and let it sit for 2-3 hours or overnight, allowing the oil to infuse with the plant's properties. After infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another bowl, discarding the solids. Now, mix the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax and shea butter melt. Remove from heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Finally, transfer the mixture to a container and let it cool completely before using the Centaurium erythraea herbal cream to soothe palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is used to help with palpitations. Palpitations are irregular heartbeats that can feel like a rapid or pounding heart. The cream contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are found in ginger. These compounds are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Centaurium erythraea herbal creams that help with palpitation, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Centaurium erythraea flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. Begin by infusing the dried flowers in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and leaving it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process helps to extract the beneficial properties of the Centaurium erythraea into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil and discard the flowers. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, then slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax while whisking continuously. Finally, add the preservative and mix well. Pour the mixture into a clean container, let it cool and solidify, and your Centaurium erythraea herbal cream is ready to use. Apply it topically to the affected area to help alleviate palpitation symptoms and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Centaurium erythraea herbal cream that helps with palpitations, you will need dried Centaurium erythraea flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, a cream base, and beeswax. First, infuse the dried flowers in the carrier oil in a clean glass jar by covering them and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Centaurium erythraea herbal creams that help with palpitation, you will need dried Centaurium erythraea flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a cream base. intro_desc: Palpitations, or irregular heartbeats, can be a distressing and frightening experience. But herbal creams can help alleviate them. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, is a natural sedative that calms the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger palpitations. Its active compound, valerenic acid, helps slow down heart rate and regulate heartbeat. Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower, is another herb that has a soothing effect on the nervous system. It contains flavonoids and alkaloids that reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which in turn can help stabilize heart rate.  Fixing palpitations is essential because they can be a symptom of a more serious underlying condition, such as heart disease or high blood pressure. Ignoring them can lead to cardiac problems, which can be life-threatening. By using herbal creams with valerian and passionflower, you can take control of your heart health and reduce your risk of cardiovascular issues. When palpitations are under control, you can enjoy a better quality of life, free from the constant fear of irregular heartbeats. You'll be able to engage in physical activities without worrying about your heart, and you'll have more energy to pursue your hobbies and passions. By taking care of your heart, you'll be able to live a healthier, happier life.
supplementary_best: A combination of Valeriana officinalis and Passiflora incarnata is effective in soothing palpitations. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, calms the nervous system and reduces anxiety. It has a sedative effect that helps slow down an overactive heart rate. Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower, has a similar sedative effect and helps reduce stress and anxiety. This combination works well because both herbs target the nervous system in a complementary way, reducing anxiety and stress that can cause palpitations. Valeriana officinalis has a more pronounced sedative effect, while Passiflora incarnata helps to stabilize the heart rate and reduce stress. Together, they provide a balanced approach to soothing palpitations and promoting relaxation.
supplementary_avoid: If you have palpitations, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. Yohimbe cream is not a good choice because yohimbe can increase heart rate and blood pressure, which can make palpitations worse. Similarly, ephedra cream is also not recommended as ephedra can raise blood pressure and heart rate, which can be problematic for people with palpitations. Another herb to avoid is guarana cream, as guarana can also increase heart rate and blood pressure.  Caffeine is found in some herbal creams, so it's best to avoid those containing caffeine, such as yerba mate cream and green tea cream, as caffeine can raise heart rate and blood pressure. Cinnamon cream may not be suitable for people with palpitations either, as large amounts of cinnamon can lower blood sugar levels too quickly, which can cause a sudden drop in blood pressure.  If you have palpitations, it's best to talk to your doctor or a qualified healthcare professional before using any herbal cream, including those that contain the herbs mentioned above. They can help you make an informed decision about which herbal creams are safe for you to use.
supplementary_other_remedies: Palpitations, or irregular heartbeats, can be caused by stress, anxiety, and other health issues. Besides herbal creams, there are other natural remedies that can help. Aromatherapy using essential oils like lavender and chamomile can calm the mind and body, reducing palpitations. These oils work by slowing down heart rate and promoting relaxation.  Ginger, often consumed as a tea or added to food, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce palpitations caused by digestive issues. Its active compound, gingerol, also helps to relax the heart muscles, promoting a steady heartbeat.  Deep breathing exercises, such as yoga and meditation, can also help manage palpitations. These exercises slow down breathing, reducing the heart's workload and promoting calmness. They also help to manage stress and anxiety, common triggers for palpitations.  A warm bath, especially with Epsom salt, can help relax the body and mind, reducing palpitations. The magnesium in Epsom salt helps to calm the nervous system, promoting a steady heartbeat.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience palpitations, or irregular heartbeats, may also experience other ailments that can be helped with medicinal herbs. For example, anxiety and stress are common causes of palpitations. The herb passionflower can help with anxiety by reducing nervous tension and promoting relaxation. It has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to slow down an irregular heartbeat.  Some people with palpitations may also experience insomnia or difficulty sleeping. The herb valerian root is known for its ability to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. It contains compounds that help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.  Palpitations can also be a symptom of low blood pressure, which can cause dizziness and lightheadedness. The herb ginseng can help to improve circulation and increase blood pressure, reducing the risk of dizziness and lightheadedness.  Additionally, people with palpitations may experience digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The herb peppermint can help to soothe digestive issues by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing inflammation.  The herb hawthorn is also known to help with palpitations by improving circulation and reducing inflammation in the heart. It has been used for centuries to help with heart health and can be taken in combination with other herbs to help manage palpitations.  These herbs work by targeting the underlying causes of palpitations, such as anxiety, stress, low blood pressure, and digestive issues. By addressing these underlying causes, these herbs can help to reduce the frequency and severity of palpitations.
